name: Daily Life Bytes
review_text:
The Central Library of IIT Delhi is a state-of-the-art facility that offers a vast collection of books, journals, and research materials. The library is equipped with modern amenities such as high-speed internet, computer workstations, and online databases, making it a valuable resource for students and researchers alike. The library also has a spacious reading room that provides a quiet and comfortable environment for studying. One of the standout features of the Central Library is its collection of rare and valuable books. The library houses several special collections, including the Mahatma Gandhi Collection, the Jawaharlal Nehru Collection, and the Rabindranath Tagore Collection. The staff at the Central Library are knowledgeable and helpful, providing assistance to students and researchers in finding the resources they need. The library also offers various workshops and training programs to help students develop their research skills.

Overall, the Central Library of IIT Delhi is an excellent resource for anyone seeking knowledge and information. Its vast collection, modern facilities, and knowledgeable staff make it an invaluable asset to the academic community. Highly recommended!

review_text:
Woah!!!
The best library I have seen.
The Library was started with the vision of P K Kelkar to make IIT the most responsible, conscious institution in an open & flexible atmosphere.

The Three storey building with huge space & serene environment is the best place to study almost in the country.
It has almost more than 3 lakhs books & over 1000 periodicals in its collection.
You will get the best periodicals, standards, reports & thesis here.
The library has been reopened with its original timings
Mon-Fri - Morning 8 to Midnight 12
Sat - Morning 9 to midnight 12
Sun & Public Holiday - closed

The scenic beauty can be imagined by the photos attached here.
The beauty is added by the fountain part nearer to it & CCD.
So, whenever you're feeling to take a break you can go to CCD & have some relaxing break.

name: BODA POOL SINGH
review_text:
Kelkar Library (Purushottam Kashinath Kelkar Library) formerly known as Central Library, is the property of Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur Spread over building covering an area of 5730 square meters and with all with modern amenities and situated in three floors located in the middle of the academic area of Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur. The Library remains open, for 358 days of the year, from 8 a.m. to 12 midnight on all working days; 9 a.m. to 12 midnight on Saturdays; 9 a.m. to 5.30 p.m. on Sundays and Gazetted holidays and for 24 hours during the two examinations each semester.
here we can find lot of information services and access to bibliographic, full text both in digital and printed resources to support the scholarly and information needs of all students, faculty members and staff.

review_text: Sweetest, chilled and best drinking water is found in P K Kelkar library as compared to the rest of IIT Kanpur. I think that when i am surrounded by the best minds(IITians) in the country, i also aspire to think big and different and maybe i can catch some thoughts flying inside the library from those IITian minds. Quiet, air conditioned and well lighted rooms in ground, 1st and 2nd floors with more than enough number of tables and chairs with charging sockets to accomodate students. Also, lightning fast Wifi and LAN ports make it very connected to the rest of the world. Also, great collection of books and journals with online subscriptions of IEEE and other online technical repositories.

name: Tanmay Saha
review_text:
The Institute has one Main Library along with many Departmental Libraries. These libraries house more than 100,000 books and subscribe to a large number of scientific and technical journals. Online access to these journals is also possible. All the students are provided with text book bank facilities where a certain number of books are issued to them for the semester. Apart from this, students also enjoy the access to the central and cyber library of Banaras Hindu University.
IIT (BHU) Varanasi has formerly been known as the Banaras Engineering College (BENCO), the College of Mining and Metallurgy (MINMET), the College of Technology (TECHNO) and the Institute of Technology, Banaras Hindu University (IT-BHU). Its establishment is intimately linked with that of the Banaras Hindu University (BHU). The first convocation ceremony at BHU was held on 19 January 1919. The Chancellor of the University, Maharaja Krishnaraja Wadiyar of Mysore who had come to preside over and address the convocation, performed the opening ceremony of the Banaras Engineering College (BENCO) Workshop buildings. An Artisan Course was started on 11 February 1919. BHU has the credit of first starting degree classes in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Metallurgy and Pharmaceutics, thanks to the foresight of its founder, Pt. Madan Mohan Malaviya.

name: Nanhe Kumar Pandey
review_text:
It was a great Experience here.
The Central Library of Indian Institute of Technology (Indian School of Mines) Dhanbad is the heart of the institute providing direct academic and research supports to all departments.

The Central Library is an automated library in terms of records organisation and management of all its different sections, search and discovery, information retrieval and service delivery. The whole library operations run on LIBSYS integrated library management software. Over the years the library has developed several databases of books, journals, theses/ dissertations, conference proceedings, etc. All documents have been bar-coded. Focus has been given on collection build-up of print resources as well as licensed e-resources plus E-Shodh Sindhu sponsored resources. The library has reached all the departments, hostels, and quarters on the campus wide LAN.

The library has initiated the development of the “Institutional Digital Repository” based on DSpace open source platform. The Central Library has set up its “Research Documentation Cell” based on the research outputs which are indexed in Scopus and Web of Science.

To meet the challenges of increasing number of students, research scholars and faculty members as well as ever changing information and communication technologies a G+7 centrally air-conditioned library having state-of-the-art facilities is being developed to provide top quality library services.

The staff of the library strive their best to help push the boundaries of IIT (ISM) Dhanbad in national and international spaces.

So Come and get some peace here.

name: PAWAN KUMAR
review_text:
The Nalanda Library at IIT Ropar stands as a beacon of knowledge and a sanctuary for avid readers. With its remarkable facilities, it has earned a reputation as one of the finest academic libraries in the region.

The library's architecture is an exquisite blend of modernity and tradition. Its central location makes it easily accessible to students and faculty alike, promoting a conducive environment for learning and research. The spacious reading hall is a serene oasis, providing a tranquil space for individuals to immerse themselves in their studies or delve into the world of literature.

The library's collection of books is extensive, catering to various disciplines and interests. The vast bank of books encompasses a plethora of subjects, catering to the diverse academic needs of the institution. Whether one seeks references for research or indulges in leisure reading, Nalanda Library is sure to satiate their thirst for knowledge.

Moreover, the library boasts state-of-the-art facilities that enhance the learning experience. Advanced digital resources, online databases, and e-books complement the physical collection, offering a seamless integration of technology into the academic landscape.

The library staff deserves commendation for their dedication and expertise in assisting users with their inquiries. Their knowledge and willingness to help create an amiable ambiance, making Naland

name: Aryan Jangra
review_text:
The library at the Indian Institute of Technology Patna (IIT Patna) is a vibrant hub of academic and intellectual activities. Spread across a spacious area, it offers a serene and conducive environment for learning and research. The library boasts a comprehensive collection of books, journals, e-resources, and research materials across diverse disciplines, catering to the varied academic needs of students and faculty members.

The digital section is particularly impressive, providing access to numerous online databases, e-books, and research journals, facilitating cutting-edge research and innovation. With its state-of-the-art infrastructure, the library is well-equipped with modern amenities, including high-speed internet, computer terminals, and comfortable reading spaces.

One of its standout features is the efficient and helpful library staff, always ready to assist users in locating resources or addressing technical issues. Regular workshops and training sessions on the use of e-resources and citation tools add further value.

However, some areas could be improved, such as extending operating hours during exam seasons and increasing the seating capacity to accommodate the growing number of users. Despite these minor limitations, the IIT Patna library remains a cornerstone of the institute's academic ecosystem, fostering a culture of knowledge and intellectual growth.

name: Sunit Das
review_text: The Central Library which is the heart of the Institute was established in 1977. It provides one of the important academic services to the Institute. It is a well-equipped Library, centrally located with easy access and does provide right impetus for the intellectual growth of the students, teachers, research scholars & others around. Presently, the Library is a part of the LTT building & having around 97,000 collections of documents of both print & non print materials.The Central Library with its modern collection of knowledge resources and innovative information services supports a complementary role for students, faculty, and the surrounding community in their intellectual pursuits. It is a hybrid library with the state-of-the-art technological applications. The Library holds knowledge resources predominantly related to Science and Technology, Humanities & Social Sc, Management & other allied subjects. The Library is now fully computerized with an integrated system connected to the Campus Network providing e-resource facility to the institutional community. The entire Library collection including the CD-ROM databases and the online databases are made available through Institute’s network. Users can access the online databases through Institute’s network. Library collection can also be searched through Web OPAC. National Institute of Technology Silchar Library is an active member of E-ShodhSindhu Consortium (to access online full text journals) and also member of NDL (National Digital Library) in order to avail the benefits of various services. In addition, library enrolled as Institutional membership of British Council Library, American Library to avail the service of ILL. Recently, NIT Silchar has invested Rs. 44 Crore to create a world-class, innovative, inviting and flexible library system to enhance campus learning, support research, and to provide community outreach service having 90,000 sq. ft. carpet area. For this purpose, Central Library, NIT Silchar has entered into a collaborative MOU with University Library & Mortenson Centre for International Library Programme of University of Illinois at Urbana Chaimpaign, USA to create world class LIS facilities at NIT Silchar. The library building is in the final stage of completion.

name: Jishan Shaikh
review_text: Old library for student, faculties, and researchers. Digital library has been opened since one year ago and it is now fully functional. Digital library has access to most premium journals, archives, and reports data from all over the worlds such as IEEE Premium, ACM access, Elsevier open access, Wiley digital, and many more. A complete and detailed list of accessed sources can be found on institute's website. Library may be old, but it is constantly getting new books for all sections. It broadly classified books and reports in 3 floors and further classified as sections. I've found all academic books in all semesters; in fact I've never bought any academic book from commercial sources. With just few work of finding a book, you get the book for 3 months. After that an overdue charge is apply for Rs 1 per book per day. And now even that charge has to pay using SBI collect. The library is also facilitating not even students of NIT, Bhopal but even that of IIIT, Bhopal also. A wide range of news papers, weekly and monthly magazines, and top reports are available in library. I particularly like Hindi section of the library, that constitutes books of importance. Same is the case for English literature. You can found books of GATE and IES (Aptitude + Engineering) preparation in ground floor, which cannot be issued but you can solve them in library itself; it has facility of studying in it also. It has several reference books also. As a matter of fact, it has more than 100,000 books available in the library. There are 3 open computers, accessing global resources available to everyone. Ah, and I forgot about Book bank also, it gave facility of issuing books (up to 6 or 20 depending on your category ;( which you can hold them to you for 1 complete year with no overdue charges. I think that's enough for now :) For more information, feel free to ask.
